criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies cherry-picking/favorable trade allocation as a distinct allegation category. It explicitly describes the pattern of disproportionately allocating profitable trades to Oceanic Fund I LP (68.4% profitable allocation rate) and Chao's personal account (81.6% profitable allocation rate) versus other client accounts (41.2% rate). The output references 'Allegation Category 1 (Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations)' multiple times, describes the statistical analysis, identifies the beneficiaries and harmed parties, and quantifies the excess profits ($7,800,000 to Oceanic Fund I LP and $438,000 to Chao's personal account).

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: Correct Statutory Provisions Cited
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly cites both Section 206(1) and Section 206(2) of the Investment Advisers Act of 1940 in connection with the cherry-picking allegation. For example, paragraph 15 states: 'the Division alleges that WCA, through the actions and omissions of Whitmore and Chao, systematically allocated same-day profitable trades to favored accounts...in violation of Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act.' Additionally, paragraphs 70 and 71 explicitly map Section 206(1) and Section 206(2) to 'Allegation Category 1 (Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations).'

criteria_results.title: Cherry-Picking: WCA Identified as Implicated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies WCA (Whitmore Capital Advisors LLC) as an implicated party in the cherry-picking allegation. Paragraph 15 of the output states: 'The Division alleges that during the Review Period, WCA, Whitmore, and Chao engaged in a pattern of favorable trade allocation commonly referred to as "cherry-picking," in violation of Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act.' WCA is named directly as a party to the cherry-picking scheme throughout the output.

criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly identifies Whitmore as implicated in the cherry-picking allegation due to his 12% LP interest in Oceanic Fund I LP. Paragraph 11 states 'Whitmore holds a 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP, representing approximately $22.2 million in value as of December 31, 2023' and paragraph 20 directly connects this financial interest to the cherry-picking motivation: 'The Division alleges that the favorable allocation to Oceanic Fund I LP was motivated by Whitmore's personal financial interest in that vehicle.' The output clearly links Whitmore's 12% LP interest to the cherry-picking scheme.

criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies Chao as implicated in the cherry-picking allegation due to her personal account receiving favorable allocations. criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does mention that Section 206(1) requires scienter while Section 206(2) does not (it notes 'Section 206(1) of the Investment Advisers Act requires proof of scienter (knowledge or intent); negligence is insufficient'), but it does not identify the corresponding point that Section 206(2) requires only negligence (a lower standard). criteria_results.title: ISSUE_006: Statute of Limitations Concern Under 28 U.S.C. Â§ 2462
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly raises the statute of limitations concern under 28 U.S.C. § 2462 in the 'Discrepancies and Defense Issues' section, stating: 'SEC civil monetary penalties are subject to the five-year statute of limitations under 28 U.S.C. § 2462. Any conduct occurring more than five years before the action is commenced may be time-barred for penalty purposes (disgorgement runs on a separate clock), so the Review-Period dates should be checked against the filing date.' The Review Period begins January 1, 2021, and the referral was made October 18, 2024, meaning early 2021 conduct is approaching or past the five-year window. The agent correctly identifies this issue and notes that disgorgement runs on a separate clock, which aligns with the Kokesh framework. The criterion is satisfied.

criteria_results.title: ISSUE_010: WCA's Respondeat Superior / Failure to Supervise Liability
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly addresses WCA's entity-level liability for Bellini's conduct beyond merely naming WCA as a respondent. This directly flags respondeat superior liability (vicarious liability for Bellini's conduct within employment scope) and failure to supervise (failure to implement adequate supervisory procedures). The output also references Section 203(e) in the context of Whitmore's supervisory failures ('potentially for supervisory failures under § 203(e)'). Additionally, the output notes that WCA's compliance review failed to detect the Lakeshore Trading arrangement, supporting the failure-to-supervise charge. These elements satisfy the criterion.

All four stated dates are accurate, and four of the five required dates are present. This meets the threshold of at least three correct dates with no incorrect dates stated. Therefore this should PASS. The output includes four of the five dates correctly (March 11, 2024; August 2, 2024; October 18, 2024; November 6, 2024), missing only the November 12, 2024 service date. Since four dates are correctly stated and none are incorrect, the criterion is satisfied.
